entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 21
entries.excerpt: The time was drawing near for Sarah to give birth; she bore Isaac and fed him at the breasts of old age. And after Isaac had been circumcised and weaned, on the day of the “great feast” which “Abraham made… on the day that Isaac was weaned” (Gen 21:8), Sarah saw Ishmael laughing. Noticing that Ishmael resembled his mother in everything, and so concluding that, as she had offended Sarah, so Ishmael would mock Isaac, she thought: “If during my life Ishmael deals so with my son, when I die will he not take a portion of my son’s inheritance, perhaps even take two portions, as the firstborn?…
